name: Tome v. United States
question: Is a prior consistent statement made after a declarant's motive to fabricate arose admissible to rebut a charge of recent fabrication or improper influence or motive under Federal Rule of Evidence 801(d)(1)(B)?
description: A case in which the Court held that Rule 801(d)(1)(B) requires that prior consistent statements be made before the motive to fabricate arose in order to be admissible.
